content.problemStatement:
A function FFF is defined on an array AAA of length NNN as follows:

F(A)=∑i=1NMEX([A1,A2,…,Ai])F(A) = \sum_{i=1}^{N} MEX([A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i])F(A)=∑i=1N​MEX([A1​,A2​,…,Ai​])

For example, if A=[2,0,1],A = [2, 0, 1],A=[2,0,1], then F(A)=MEX([2])+MEX([2,0])F(A) = MEX([2]) + MEX([2,0])F(A)=MEX([2])+MEX([2,0]) +MEX([2,0,1]) + MEX([2,0,1])+MEX([2,0,1]) =0+1+3=4 = 0 +1 +3 = 4=0+1+3=4.

You are given two integers NNN and KKK, with N≤KN \leq KN≤K. For each i=N,N+1,…,Ki = N, N + 1, \dots, Ki=N,N+1,…,K in order, find the number of permutations PPP of integers {0,1,2,…,N−1}\{0, 1, 2, \dots, N - 1\}{0,1,2,…,N−1} such that F(P)=iF(P) = iF(P)=i. Since the answers can be very large, find them modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).

Recall that the mex of an array is the smallest non-negative integer that does not belong to the array. For example, MEX([3,2,1])=0MEX([3, 2, 1]) = 0MEX([3,2,1])=0, MEX([3,1,0,1])=2MEX([3, 1, 0, 1]) = 2MEX([3,1,0,1])=2.


Input Format

The first and only line of input contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format

For each i  (N≤i≤K)i \; (N \leq i \leq K)i(N≤i≤K), print on a single line the number of permutations PPP, modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7) with F(P)=iF(P) = iF(P)=i.
